name: branch-buddy
description: Turn a VS Code window to a custom color when any other open VS Code instance is on a different git branch.

commands.title: Branch Buddy: Show status of all instances
Branch Buddy: Set group for this workspace

branchBuddy.group.description: Project group identifier. Only VS Code instances that share the same non-empty group are compared against each other. Set this in workspace settings for each repo that belongs to the same project (e.g. "acme-app" in both the frontend and backend repos). When empty, Branch Sync is inactive for this window.

branchBuddy.color.description: Background color used for the title/status/activity bar when a branch mismatch is detected.

branchBuddy.foreground.description: Foreground color used alongside the mismatch alert color.

branchBuddy.groupColor.description: Foreground color for the group name shown in the status bar (CSS color string, e.g. "#7aa2f7"). Leave empty to inherit the theme's default status bar color.

markdownEnumDescriptions: Track only the first workspace folder. This preserves the original one-entry-per-VS-Code-window behavior.
Track every workspace folder as its own registry entry. Use this for multi-root workspaces where projects in the same sync group should be compared inside the same VS Code window too.
branchBuddy.trackingMode.description: Controls which repositories Branch Sync tracks in a VS Code window.

branchBuddy.heartbeatMs.description: How often (ms) this instance updates its entry in the shared registry.

branchBuddy.staleMs.description: Registry entries older than this (ms) are treated as dead instances and ignored.

scripts.prepare:integration: npm run fixture:integration && npm run compile && npm run compile:integration
scripts.coverage:unit: vitest run --config vitest.config.ts --coverage && npm run coverage:copy:unit
scripts.coverage:copy:unit: node -e "const fs=require('fs');if(!fs.existsSync('coverage/unit')){console.log('[coverage] coverage/unit not present, skipping copy');process.exit(0);}if(!fs.existsSync('/coverage-out'))fs.mkdirSync('/coverage-out',{recursive:true});fs.rmSync('/coverage-out/unit',{recursive:true,force:true});fs.cpSync('coverage/unit','/coverage-out/unit',{recursive:true});console.log('[coverage] copied unit report -> /coverage-out/unit/ (on host: ./coverage/unit/)');"
scripts.coverage:integration: npm run prepare:integration && vscode-test --coverage --coverage-output coverage/integration && npm run coverage:copy:integration
scripts.coverage:copy:integration: node -e "const fs=require('fs');if(!fs.existsSync('coverage/integration')){console.log('[coverage] coverage/integration not present, skipping copy');process.exit(0);}if(!fs.existsSync('/coverage-out'))fs.mkdirSync('/coverage-out',{recursive:true});fs.rmSync('/coverage-out/integration',{recursive:true,force:true});fs.cpSync('coverage/integration','/coverage-out/integration',{recursive:true});console.log('[coverage] copied integration report -> /coverage-out/integration/ (on host: ./coverage/integration/)');"
scripts.coverage: npm run coverage:unit && npm run coverage:integration
